disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No surgical-abdomen features — no severe constant or localised right-lower-quadrant pain, no rigid/guarded abdomen, cramps ease after passing stool, still passing diarrhoea (argues against appendicitis/obstruction)
trigger_question_examples: Is the pain becoming severe, constant and localised (e.g. right lower tummy), or crampy and easing after you pass stool?
Is your tummy rigid/very tender, and have you stopped passing wind/stool?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's crampy and eases after I go; not severe/constant or one-sided, my tummy isn't rigid, and I'm still passing diarrhoea.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No invasive/bacterial-diarrhoea features — no blood/mucus in stool, no high sustained fever/toxicity, no recent travel/antibiotics — and dehydration risk from vomiting (living alone)
trigger_question_examples: Any blood or mucus in the stool, or a high sustained fever, and any recent travel or antibiotics?
How much are you keeping down and passing urine?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No blood or mucus, only a mild fever earlier, no travel or antibiotics; I'm keeping almost nothing down but still passing some urine.
